structured.description: A function `calibrate4` that takes a robot ID as input and uses it to calibrate a kinova camera using a square error function. The function first defines the necessary parameters for the calibration, such as the camera's intrinsic parameters, and then minimizes the square of the difference between the detected corners in the real image and the virtual image using the Nelder-Mead method to find the optimal optical center. Finally, it prints the final optical center and error value.

items.description: Performs a calibration process for a robotic arm using a cube as a reference frame to determine the camera's intrinsic parameters and extrinsic parameters for a specific robot ID.

attributes.name: width
attributes.description: 1280 pixels, representing the width of the image captured by the camera.

attributes.name: error
attributes.description: Calculated as a sum of absolute values of errors between real corners and detected virtual corners using the `error_function4_cube` method. It represents the overall calibration error.

attributes.name: real_corners
attributes.description: 4-dimensional, representing the corners of a real object in the image.

attributes.name: robot_id
attributes.description: Used to store the ID of the robot being calibrated.

attributes.name: observation_angles
attributes.description: 7-dimensional, representing the angular positions of the camera's lens at each corner detection.

items.name: Calibrator

items.description: Is used to initialize an object of the `Calibrator` class, by setting its properties such as `observation_angles` and `model_params` based on user-input parameters.
items.name: __init__

items.description: Within the `Calibrator` class determines if an approximation of a rectangle can be found based on the cosine of the angles between adjacent points. If all four angles are within a threshold (0.1), the approximation is considered a rectangle, and the function returns `True`. Otherwise, it returns `False`.

params.name: approx
params.description: Used to determine if an given shape is a rectangle based on its angles.

returns.description: True if the input approximation is a rectangle, and False otherwise.

items.name: is_rectangle

items.description: Calculates the angle between two points, based on their distances and angles from a reference point.

params.name: pt1
params.description: Represented as `[x, y]`.

params.name: pt2
params.description: Defined as a tuple containing two elements, (x, y), representing the second point on the line segment between point 1 and point 0.

params.name: pt0
params.description: Represented by the tuple `(x0, y0)`.

returns.description: A measure of the angle between two points in a 2D plane, calculated using the cosine of the angle between them.

items.name: angle

items.description: Within a `Calibrator` class detects green corners in an image by converting it to HSV color space, defining lower and upper green ranges, applying thresholding, and finding contours using the moments of the contour.

params.name: image
params.description: Used to represent an input image for green corner detection.

returns.description: 3-element list containing the (x, y) coordinates of the detected green corners in the original image.

items.name: detect_green_corners

items.description: Within the Calibrator class, calculates errors between real and virtual corners detected from an image using the SURF detector. It returns the sum of these errors.

params.name: params
params.description: 7-dimensional, containing the following elements: com_p, com_o, fov, camera_matrix, dist_coeffs, focal_length, and exposure.

items.name: error_function

items.description: Calculates the distance between real and virtual corners, checks for errors, and updates the errors variable with the norm of the difference between the two sets.

params.name: params
params.description: 7 elements long, containing values for camera intrinsic parameters and focal length.

returns.description: Not a standard value. It may indicate an error or exception occurred during the execution of the code.

items.name: error_function2

items.description: Calculates the distance between real and virtual corners, checks for collisions, and updates the errors variable with the sum of the distances.

params.name: params
params.description: Used to store the translation and rotation matrix of a camera.

returns.description: 2000 when there are fewer than 10 real corners matched to virtual corners, and a sum of errors between the real and virtual corners.

items.name: error_function3

items.description: Performs camera calibration for both Pybullet and Kinova cameras by detecting rectangles in the images, computing offsets, and updating camera parameters.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: Used to store the input image for calibration.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the robot to be calibrated.

items.name: calibrate

items.description: Performs camera calibration for a Kinova robot using a green square detected in an image. It minimizes an error function to estimate the camera matrix, distortion coefficients, and FOV.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description:
Used to store an image captured by the camera attached to the Kinova robot, which is used 
to detect green corners for calibration.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to specify the robot's unique identifier for state estimation.

items.name: calibrate2

items.description: In the Calibrator class takes an image and robot pose, computes the fixed projection matrix, and minimizes the error between the robot pose and the corners detected in the image using the Nelder-Mead algorithm.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify a specific robot in a fleet.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: An image from the camera used for detecting corners.

items.name: calibrate3

items.description: Computes the view and projection matrices based on the camera's field of view, aspect ratio, near plane, and far plane. It then rotates the image and converts it to a grayscale format for further processing.

returns.description: A numpy array with shape (1, 3, 768, 480) containing the rotated camera image.

items.name: read_camera

items.description: Calculates the fixed projection and view matrices for a camera based on its intrinsic and extrinsic parameters, and returns the RGB image captured by the camera.

returns.description: 3D numpy array with shape ( height, width, 3) representing a grayscale image.

items.name: read_camera_fixed

items.description: Within a class `Calibrator` performs image processing tasks, including green corner detection and matching with real corners, to calculate the errors between virtual and real corners.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify a specific robot in the code.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: A representation of an image captured by a camera mounted on a kinova robot.

items.name: cube_test

items.description: Within a Calibrator class performs rectangle detection on both PyBullet and Kinova camera images using Gaussian blur, Canny edge detection, contour finding, and circle drawing around detected rectangles. It also computes the distance between real and virtual corners and displays errors.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the robot for which the square detection algorithm is being executed.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description:
Used to display the rectangular region detected on the Kinova camera image after applying 
the Canny edge detector.

items.name: square_test

items.description: Within the Calibrator class enables the robot to capture images from a Kinova arm and a camera, and saves them as square-shaped color images with file names based on their index value.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the robot for which images are being captured.

params.name: kinova_proxy
params.description: Used to manipulate the joint angles of a Kinova arm.

params.name: camera_proxy
params.description: Used to retrieve RGBD images from a camera.

items.name: get_kinova_images

items.description: Calculates the distance between a set of virtual corners and real corners, and returns the total error. It takes in a list of parameters, including optical center x and y pixels, and projects them onto a bullet-like robot using PyBullet library.

params.name: params
params.description: 2-dimensional, containing two elements: the optical center x pixels and optical center y pixels.

returns.description: The sum of the absolute differences between the virtual corners and real corners.

items.name: error_function4_square

items.description: Calculates the distance between real and virtual corners in a 3D scene, checks for errors, and returns an overall error value.

params.name: params
params.description: 2D, containing two elements: `optical_center_x_pixels` and `optical_center_y_pixels`.

returns.description: An error value calculated by comparing the real corners and virtual corners of a cube.

items.name: error_function4_cube

items.description: Minimizes an error function to determine the optimal position of a robot's optical center using a Nelder-Mead algorithm.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to assign a unique identifier to each robot.

items.name: calibrate4

structured.description:
A robot arm with 7 joints and uses the PyRobot library to interact with a Robot Operating System (ROS) environment. It reads sensor data from the robot's end effector and calculates the inverse kinematics solution to reach a target position. The code also sets motor control positions for each joint using the `setJointMotorControl2` function from the PyRobot library.

Overall, the code is designed to control the movement of a robot arm using sensor data and inverse kinematics calculations, with the goal of reaching a desired target position.

items.description: Manages the movement of a robotic arm based on sensor data from a vision system and user input. It calculates the target position and orientation of the arm's end effector and sends control signals to the arm's motors to reach the desired position and orientation.

attributes.name: Period
attributes.description: 5 by default, representing the time step between movements in seconds. It determines how often the worker will move based on the current mode.

attributes.name: rgb
attributes.description: 3-dimensional array, representing the color of the worker's body in RGB format.

attributes.name: startup_check
attributes.description: 0-indexed, indicating which state the worker is in during startup (0 for home position, 1 for move mode, etc.).

attributes.name: physicsClient
attributes.description: Used to interact with the PyMel physics engine to perform actions such as moving objects, rotating them, or changing their position.

attributes.name: plane
attributes.description: Used to store the target position and orientation of a plane in the robot's workspace, which can be manipulated using the buttons and axes inputs.

attributes.name: table_id
attributes.description: Used to identify a specific worker in a table of workers, allowing the robot to perform different actions based on which worker it is.

attributes.name: robot_urdf
attributes.description: A file path to a Robot URDF file for a specific worker, which defines the robot's geometry and kinematics.

attributes.name: robot_launch_pos
attributes.description: A list containing the position of the robot before launch, including the x, y, and z coordinates of the robot's origin.

attributes.name: robot_launch_orien
attributes.description: 3D rotation matrix representing the orientation of the robot's end effector.

attributes.name: end_effector_link_index
attributes.description: 0-based indexing for the end effector links of the robot, indicating which link the end effector is attached to.

attributes.name: home_angles
attributes.description: Used to store the angles for each axis in home position, which are used as references for the robot's movement.

attributes.name: observation_angles
attributes.description: 3D angles (in radians) representing the current observation of the robot, used to determine the robot's pose relative to its environment.

attributes.name: observation_angles_2
attributes.description: Used to store the angles for the robot's end effector (gripper) in the second observation.

attributes.name: observation_angles_3
attributes.description: 3D angles representing the orientation of the robot's end effector, used for calculating the target position and orientation of the robot's end effector based on user input.

attributes.name: observation_angles_cube
attributes.description: A list of angles for each axis (X, Y, Z) of the cube-shaped observation space. It defines the orientation of the worker's end effector in the workspace.

attributes.name: robot_id
attributes.description: 4 by default, indicating that this worker is the fourth one in a list of workers. It is used to identify the specific robot being controlled by the worker.

attributes.name: cup
attributes.description: Used to store the current state of the robot's end effector position and orientation in a specific workspace.

attributes.name: square
attributes.description: 4 by default, which represents the step size for the robot's movement in the X, Y, and Z axes.

attributes.name: hilo_lectura
attributes.description: A function that reads data from a file using the HIL (Hardware-in-the-Loop) format, specifically for the robotic arm.

attributes.name: readDataFromProxy
attributes.description: Used to read data from a proxy server. It takes no arguments and returns a tuple containing the data as (position, orientation, buttons) for the given mode.

attributes.name: hilo_camara
attributes.description: Used to store the position and orientation of the end effector in a specific frame of reference, specifically the "camera" frame.

attributes.name: readCamera
attributes.description:
Not defined by default. It is used to read camera data from a given camera index or name, which can be 
a string or integer value.

attributes.name: target_angles
attributes.description: Used to store the target angles for the robot's axes, which are calculated based on the data received from the controller.

attributes.name: target_position
attributes.description: Used to store the target position of the robot's end effector for a specific task, such as moving an object from one location to another.

attributes.name: target_orientation
attributes.description: Used to store the target orientation of the robot after a move command has been applied, it's updated by the `getEulerFromQuaternion()` method.

attributes.name: target_velocities
attributes.description: Used to store the target velocities for each joint of the robot in the current mode.

attributes.name: joy_selected_joint
attributes.description: 0-based index of the selected joint to be controlled based on the input from the joystick.

attributes.name: move_mode
attributes.description: 0-based indexed, changing its value based on the buttons' steps in each mode (home, move, or 3rd party).

attributes.name: n_rotations
attributes.description: 0-based index representing the number of rotations to apply to the robot's end effector to reach the target position. It is used in conjunction with the `move_mode` attribute to determine the motion of the robot.

attributes.name: ext_joints
attributes.description: 0-indexed, containing the joints of the robot beyond the primary joints (X, Y, Z) for the worker.

attributes.name: kinovaarm_proxy
attributes.description: Used to store a Python proxy for communicating with the Kinova Arm robotic arm.

attributes.name: ext_gripper
attributes.description: 20 characters long, representing a gripper extension that can be adjusted for specific tasks.

attributes.name: posesTimes
attributes.description: 3D array containing the position, orientation, and timestamp of the robot's end effector for each step of a given task. It is used to store the state of the robot over time during the execution of the task.

attributes.name: poses
attributes.description: 3D position vectors for the robot's end effector, representing its current position and orientation in the workspace.

attributes.name: timestamp
attributes.description: Representing the current timestamp in milliseconds since the epoch (January 1, 1970, 00:00:00 UTC). It is used to track the time elapsed during a task execution.

attributes.name: inicializar_toolbox
attributes.description: Used to initialize the toolbox of the worker with the correct tool for the current operation. It takes no arguments and returns none.

attributes.name: timer
attributes.description: 0 by default, indicating that the worker is not timing out. It tracks the number of seconds the worker has been running without a break or interruption.

attributes.name: compute
attributes.description: Used to compute the next position and orientation of the robot based on the input data from the user.

attributes.name: timer2
attributes.description: 2 seconds by default, indicating how often the worker will check for new commands from the robot's control panel.

attributes.name: movePybulletWithExternalVel
attributes.description: Used to move the robot using PyBullet's `move()` function, while also applying external torque.

attributes.name: joint_speeds
attributes.description: 4D, where each element in the array corresponds to a joint and has a value representing its speed in radians per second. It is used to control the movement of the robot's joints during a motion plan.

attributes.name: gains
attributes.description: Used to control the speed and acceleration of the robot's movements.

attributes.name: speeds
attributes.description: 4-dimensional, representing the desired speeds for each axis of the robot's end effector in meters per second.

attributes.name: angles
attributes.description: Used to store the angles of each joint of the robot in radians.

attributes.name: timer3
attributes.description: 3, indicating that the worker has a timer with a value of 3.

attributes.name: moveKinovaWithSpeeds
attributes.description: 4-dimensional, representing the desired position and orientation of the Kinova robot's end effector in a Cartesian coordinate system.

attributes.name: timer4
attributes.description: 4, indicating that the worker is a specific type of worker with a particular set of axes and buttons.

attributes.name: movePybulletWithToolbox
attributes.description: Used to move the robot using PyBullet, a Python library for physics-based simulation and visualization. It takes in a list of data from the `data` attribute and moves the robot according to that data.

attributes.name: colorKinova
attributes.description: Used to store the color of the Kinova robot's end effector.

attributes.name: depthKinova
attributes.description: 0, indicating that the worker is a Kinova robot.

attributes.name: calibrator
attributes.description: Used to store a calibration object for the robot's end effector poses.

attributes.name: timer5
attributes.description: 5 seconds in length, used to control the speed of the robot's movement.

attributes.name: readKinovaCamera
attributes.description: Used to read the camera data from the Kinova robot's onboard camera.

items.name: SpecificWorker

items.description: Initializes the SpecificWorker class, setting up timers for moving the robot arm and reading the camera, as well as defining the joint speeds and angles based on the target positions and orientations.

params.name: proxy_map
params.description: Used to map PyBullet joint names to Kinova arm joint names. It allows for customizing the mapping between the two frameworks, which can be useful in case of mismatches or specific requirements.

params.name: startup_check
params.description: 0 by default, indicating that the worker has finished its startup check and is ready to process tasks.

items.name: __init__

items.description: Sets parameters for an instance of the `SpecificWorker` class, which is a subclass of `GenericWorker`. The function returns `True` upon successful parameter setting.

params.name: params
params.description: Used to set parameters for an application.

returns.description: A standard return value for a successful method execution.

items.name: setParams

items.description:
Performs various tasks, including:

* Moving the robot to specific angles using the `moveKinovaWithAngles` method
* Calculating and storing the observation angles for the gripper
* Running the calibrator to update the robot's parameters
* Processing images from the YOLO detector
* Calibrating the robot's end effector using AAMED.
items.name: compute

items.description: Tests various components and interfaces of a RoboCompKinovaArm, including TPose, TGripper, TJoint, TJoints, AxisParams, ButtonParams, and TData. It also calls the QApplication.instance().quit() method after 200 milliseconds to quit the application.
items.name: startup_check

items.description: Initializes the worker's toolbox, setting up kinematic and robotic components, adding objects to the environment, and defining various variables for subsequent use.
items.name: inicializar_toolbox

items.description: Computes and updates the joint velocities for a robot based on user inputs, and also computes the error between the desired joint angles and the actual joint angles of the robot.
items.name: toolbox_compute

items.description: Computes and applies a view matrix to obtain an image from a camera, then displays the resulting RGB image using OpenCV.
items.name: readCamera

items.description: Reads data from a Kinova camera, extracts color and depth information, and displays the results in real-time using OpenCV.
items.name: readKinovaCamera

items.description: Computes and prints the radians of the joint angles of a kinova robot, using the `ext_joints` attribute of the class `SpecificWorker`.
items.name: showKinovaAngles

items.description: Calibrates a camera for kinova-specific intrinsic parameters using a chessboard pattern. It captures images, detects corners, and computes the matrix and distortion coefficients. Finally, it saves the results to a file.

returns.description: 1 if the calibration was successful, and 0 otherwise.

items.name: get_kinova_instrinsic

items.description: Provides a list of actions that can be taken from a displayed window: taking a picture, computing calibration, resetting the program, and quitting.
items.name: usage

items.description: Updates the velocity of the robot's joints based on external velocities provided by an external joint controller, scaling the target velocities to account for radial distance from the origin.
items.name: movePybulletWithExternalVel

items.description: Controls the velocity of a robot using PyBullet, setting joint motor control inputs for each robot arm based on target velocities provided in the function call.
items.name: movePybulletWithToolbox

items.description: Reads data from a Kinova arm proxy and updates the values of external gripper distance to 80% of its original value before sleeping for 0.05 seconds.
items.name: readDataFromProxy

items.description: Converts angles into degrees and rounds them to the nearest degree, then passes the result to the `moveJointsWithAngle` method of the `kinovaarm_proxy` instance, which moves the robot's joints based on the provided angles.

params.name: angles
params.description: 1D, representing the joint angles in radians to move the Kinova arm.

items.name: moveKinovaWithAngles

items.description: Calculates joint speeds for a Kinova arm based on joint angles and gains, and sends the speeds to the Kinova arm proxy for movement.
items.name: moveKinovaWithSpeeds

items.description: Updates the kinematic gains for each joint based on the difference between the actual and target angles, and prints the gains and error messages to the console.
items.name: updateGains

items.description: Receives input data from a joystick and updates the robot's position, orientation, and mode based on the received axes and buttons values.

params.name: data
params.description: Passed the joystick data from the user, containing the axis and button values.

items.name: JoystickAdapter_sendData
